commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-neutral validation for delegated run-session handles before writing lease output and adds CLI/all-provider tests for malformed handles and run-session feature conformance.

Reproducibility: no. executed reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection shows current main can write a non-nil delegated run session without first validating the required provider, lease ID, and cleanup command fields.
commands.review_followup: - This member-authored PR has no narrow automated repair; maintainer review should decide the fail-closed contract timing and merge after required checks.

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics via onHiddenDiagnostic callback
target.body:
## Summary

Empty or malformed plugin tool `availability` groups (e.g. `{ anyOf: [] }`, `{ allOf: ["bad"] }`) silently hid the tool from the plan with no diagnostic. This adds an `onHiddenDiagnostic` callback so `resolvePluginTools` can surface those as authoring warnings, and preserves plugin-authored availability metadata so the planner can evaluate and warn instead of dropping it silently.

Fixes #92361.

## Current diff (warn-only, 6 source files)

- `src/tools/types.ts` — add `onHiddenDiagnostic?` to `BuildToolPlanOptions`.
- `src/tools/planner.ts` — invoke `onHiddenDiagnostic` for each diagnostic that hides a tool.
- `src/tools/availability.ts` — replace the key-presence `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` with `isValidSignalShape`, which validates `kind` plus the required fields per signal type and rejects non-array `allOf`/`anyOf`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts` — `readAgentToolAvailability` preserves availability-like records so malformed groups still reach the planner/warn path instead of being dropped at capture.
- `src/plugins/tools.ts` — wire `buildToolPlan` + `onHiddenDiagnostic` into `resolvePluginTools`; guard `collectAuthProviderIds`; resolve manifest control-plane enabled plugin ids.
- `src/plugins/manifest-contract-eligibility.ts` — `resolveManifestControlPlaneEnabledPluginIds` helper.

**Not in this diff:** there are no `src/plugin-sdk/*` changes — an earlier SDK-export iteration was dropped. The current behavior is warn-only diagnostics, not a fail-closed/SDK-contract change.

## Crash guard (addresses the P1 review finding)

`collectAuthProviderIds` walks nested `allOf`/`anyOf` entries, which — because `readAgentToolAvailability` intentionally preserves malformed records for the planner to warn on — can be primitives or `null`. It now returns early on non-object/`null` before any `in` check:

```ts
if (!availability || typeof availability !== "object") {
  return;
}
```

so `{ allOf: ["bad"] }` / `{ anyOf: [null] }` no longer throw before diagnostics are emitted. A follow-up commit also guards self-referential availability cycles.

Regression coverage: `resolves tools without throwing when availability holds malformed nested entries` drives `{ allOf: ["bad", null] }` through `resolvePluginTools` with `hasAuthForProvider: () => true` (the exact crash path the review flagged).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** empty/malformed availability groups were hidden with no diagnostic; malformed nested entries could additionally crash `resolvePluginTools` during auth-provider collection. After this change the planner emits an authoring diagnostic and the collector no longer throws.

**Environment tested:** Node v22.22.0 on Linux, branch `fix/92361-tool-availability-empty-group-warning`, head `b49789bc3f90fceea666ef6b9d138f3f5bbc0465`.

**Evidence after fix:**

`isToolAvailabilityExpression` validation:

```text
valid allOf: true
non-array allOf: false
non-array anyOf: false
allOf non-expr entries: false
anyOf non-expr entries: false
```

`resolvePluginTools` with empty `anyOf` (authoring warning surfaced, not silently hidden):

```text
empty group tools=
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
```

**Verification on head `b49789bc3f9`:**

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/tools/availability.test.ts
  -> 100 passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
  -> exit 0
```

**What was not tested:** live gateway startup against a real external plugin package shipping malformed availability metadata.

## Compatibility note

This starts preserving plugin-authored `availability` metadata that current `main` and `v2026.6.9` ignore during descriptor capture, surfaced as warn-only diagnostics. Hidden tools behave as before; the only added behavior is the diagnostic warning. Maintainers should explicitly accept the warn-only semantics (or select an overlapping sibling implementation for #92361).
target.author: pick-cat
